message:
fix(extensions): correct path.join absolute segment handling and eval false positives

Two independent bug fixes in the extension runtime:

1. pi.path.join() absolute path handling (extensions_js.rs):
   The __pi_path_join shim had Node.js-style behavior where an absolute segment
   (starting with '/') would reset the accumulated path, so join('/a', '/b')
   returned '/b' instead of '/a/b'. This diverged from the pi-mono TS runtime
   behavior where segments are always concatenated. Removed the startsWith('/')
   reset branch so join('/a', '/b') now correctly returns '/a/b'. Added a
   dedicated test (pijs_pi_path_join_behavior) covering absolute concat, normal
   join, root join, and dot-dot traversal.

2. eval() call detection (extension_preflight.rs):
   contains_eval_call() used is_ascii_alphanumeric() to check the character
   before "eval(", which missed underscore (_) and dollar ($) — both valid JS
   identifier characters. This caused false positives on identifiers like
   "my_eval('code')" and "$eval('code')". Changed to is_js_ident_continue()
   which properly recognizes all JS identifier continuation characters. Added
   two targeted tests for underscore and dollar prefix identifiers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
